task_completion.description: Labeled shapes for all required plumbing fixtures are present in the draw.io canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io Desktop application is open, and the canvas contains multiple shapes with text labels corresponding to plumbing fixtures (e.g., City Water Main, Water Meter, Water Heater, Sinks, Toilets, Showers).

task_completion.description: Blue lines representing cold water supply connect the source to the fixtures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blue lines are drawn connecting the City Water Main / Meter to the Water Heater and other fixtures in the house.

task_completion.description: Red lines representing hot water supply connect the water heater to the appropriate fixtures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Red lines are drawn originating from the Water Heater and connecting to fixtures like sinks and showers.

task_completion.description: The connections adhere to standard plumbing logic for hot and cold water.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Toilets are connected ONLY to blue (cold) lines. The Water Heater has a blue line entering it and a red line exiting it. Sinks, showers, and washing machines have both blue and red lines connected to them.

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved as a .drawio file and exported as a .png image to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save/export dialogs show the files being saved as 'plumbing_schematic.drawio' and 'plumbing_schematic.png' to the Desktop directory, or these files are visibly present on the Desktop background or in a file manager.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io Desktop graphical user interface to create the schematic.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the draw.io Desktop application open with the diagram being actively edited, rather than terminal commands generating XML or image files directly.

integrity.description: The agent manually constructed the diagram by adding shapes and lines.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the progressive addition of shapes, text labels, and colored lines within the draw.io canvas over time.

integrity.description: The agent did not download a pre-existing plumbing schematic from the internet.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent's browser history or active windows do not show image search results for plumbing schematics being downloaded and passed off as the final product.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The specification file is located at /home/ga/Desktop/plumbing_specs.txt and contains 11 fixtures for the 'Miller Residence'. Note that the actual labels in the diagram should reflect the specific names in the spec file (e.g., 'City Water Main (Source)', 'Water Meter', 'Water Heater (Tankless)'), not just generic names. Cold water lines must be blue (e.g., #0000FF, #0000CC) and hot water lines must be red (e.g., #FF0000, #CC0000). The final outputs must be saved exactly as /home/ga/Desktop/plumbing_schematic.drawio and /home/ga/Desktop/plumbing_schematic.png.
